description: Port the pre-script feature from the poc/pre-script-feature branch (commit 990d64cb) into main as a production-grade platform primitive. A pre-script is plugin code that runs at document_start in MAIN world via chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts, strictly before any page script. This lets plugins observe or patch page runtime state (auth tokens, CSRF nonces, early globals, fetch/XHR) that the page would otherwise hide — solving the PR #69 Outlook/cloud.microsoft class of failures where MSAL encrypts localStorage at rest and the page overwrites window.fetch before ordinary adapters can load. The POC proved the mechanism end-to-end; this PRD brings it to merge-ready quality. Scope: (1) port the core plumbing verbatim from the POC — shared constants, SDK writer/reader subpath exports, build-tool pre-script bundling, MCP-server loader/adapter-files/state/extension-protocol threading, extension registration module and message-router wiring; (2) close the three production gaps identified during POC review — re-register pre-scripts on extension install/startup/update so persistAcrossSessions gaps don't strand plugins, validate the preScriptFile wire field against a safe filename pattern to prevent path-traversal, and include the pre-script source in plugin_inspect so the plugin review system cannot be bypassed by shipping un-reviewed pre-script code; (3) surface hasPreScript on the side panel PluginCard so users can see at a glance which plugins run code at document_start in MAIN world; (4) auto-reload matching tabs when a plugin's preScriptHash changes via plugin.update so already-open tabs get the new script without manual reload; (5) wire removePreScript into the plugin.uninstall path so registrations don't leak; (6) replace the POC e2e test with a production-grade suite that uses the main-branch fixtures (setupIsolatedIifeTest, startTestServer-style shape) and covers not just the happy-path bearer capture but also: stale pre-script cleanup on plugin uninstall, re-registration after extension restart, auto-reload on preScriptHash change, and rejection of malformed preScriptFile payloads; (7) document the feature in plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md, browser-extension/CLAUDE.md, mcp-server/CLAUDE.md. The POC plugin plugins/prescript-test and its mock server e2e/prescript-mock-server.ts are ported as-is (they are already well-scoped) but the POC e2e test e2e/prescript-poc.e2e.ts is discarded and rewritten using fixtures. After all stories land, the platform has a reviewed, documented, extension-lifecycle-correct pre-script primitive that plugin authors can adopt for any site whose page JavaScript hides state the adapter would need. The poc/pre-script-feature branch is superseded and can be deleted after merge.

userStories.title: Add PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME constant and manifest.preScript field to @opentabs-dev/shared
userStories.description: Port the shared-package pieces of the POC verbatim: PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME constant in constants.ts, preScript field in the PluginOpentabsField interface, its parser branch in parsePluginPackageJson, and the re-export in the barrel.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/shared/src/constants.ts exports const PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME = 'pre-script.iife.js'
platform/shared/src/index.ts re-exports PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME from constants.js alphabetically (between PLUGIN_PREFIX and pluginNameFromPackage)
PluginOpentabsField in platform/shared/src/manifest.ts has readonly preScript?: string
parsePluginPackageJson rejects a non-string or empty-string preScript with 'opentabs.preScript must be a non-empty string path'
parsePluginPackageJson returns parsedPreScript in the ok() result only when the input had one (conditional spread, matches sibling fields like configSchema)

userStories.title: Add @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script subpath export with definePreScript + SDK getPreScriptValue reader
userStories.description: Port platform/plugin-sdk/src/pre-script.ts (writer API) and platform/plugin-sdk/src/pre-script-reader.ts (adapter-side reader) from the POC and wire them into package.json exports and the SDK barrel. These files are final shapes — copy verbatim from poc/pre-script-feature@990d64cb.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-sdk/src/pre-script.ts exports const definePreScript, type PreScriptContext, type PreScriptValue
platform/plugin-sdk/src/pre-script-reader.ts exports getPreScriptValue<T>(key): T | undefined
platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts re-exports getPreScriptValue in the SDK-utilities section above Logging

userStories.title: Extend opentabs-plugin build to bundle pre-script IIFE and bind _pluginName in adapter handlers
userStories.description: Port the build-tool changes from the POC so that when a plugin declares opentabs.preScript in its package.json, the build emits dist/pre-script.iife.js (separate from dist/adapter.iife.js) and records preScriptFile + preScriptHash in dist/tools.json. Also port the adapter wrapper tweak that pins globalThis.__openTabs._pluginName to the current plugin before invoking each tool handler — without this, getPreScriptValue reads the wrong bucket when multiple plugins ship adapters into the same tab.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts imports PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME from @opentabs-dev/shared
runBuild calls bundlePreScript when pkgJson.opentabs.preScript is set, after resolveSdkVersion and before generateManifest
bundlePreScript writes a temp wrapper, runs esbuild with format:'iife', platform:'browser', bundle:true, sourcemap:'linked', and deletes the temp wrapper in finally

The pre-script wrapper injects PLUGIN_NAME via JSON.stringify at build time, resets globalThis.__openTabs.preScript[PLUGIN_NAME] on each load, sets globalThis.__openTabs._preScriptRunner, and deletes _preScriptRunner after the user module import resolves (success or failure)
generateManifest includes preScriptFile and preScriptHash in the output when bundlePreScript produced a file
preScriptHash is computed as sha256 hex of the IIFE content with the sourceMappingURL trailer stripped (same pattern the adapter uses)
The tool.handle wrapper in the adapter IIFE generator sets `runtime._pluginName = ${JSON.stringify(name)}` before calling onToolInvocationStart
OpenTabsRuntime in build.ts declares _pluginName?: string and preScript?: Record<string, Record<string, unknown>>
Build fails with a clear error mentioning package.json when opentabs.preScript points to a non-existent source file
PluginManifestOutput interface gains optional preScriptFile and preScriptHash string fields
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts (add bundlePreScript helper, extend PluginManifestOutput, extend OpenTabsRuntime, extend generateManifest, extend runBuild, tweak the tool.handle wrap to pin _pluginName)

Context: The POC's build.ts changes (133 insertions, 8 deletions) are the meat of the plugin-tools work. All of it ports verbatim — it is a well-thought-out IIFE wrapper already tested by the POC Playwright run.

Read first: git show poc/pre-script-feature -- platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts

Changes — imports:
The top of build.ts imports from @opentabs-dev/shared. Add PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME to the existing block (alphabetical):
Existing:
```ts
import {
  ...,
  DEFAULT_PORT,
  getConfigDir,
  getConfigPath,
  parsePluginPackageJson,
  pluginNameFromPackage,
  TOOLS_FILENAME,
  ...
} from '@opentabs-dev/shared';
```
Insert `PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME,` between `getConfigPath,` and `parsePluginPackageJson,`.

Changes — PluginManifestOutput (around line 624):
Current interface has `iconInactiveSvg?: string` etc. Add two fields:
```ts
  preScriptFile?: string;
  preScriptHash?: string;
```

Changes — generateManifest signature and body (around line 693):
Existing:
```ts
const generateManifest = (plugin: OpenTabsPlugin, sdkVersion: string, icons?: IconResult): PluginManifestOutput => ({
  sdkVersion,
  ...(plugin.configSchema && Object.keys(plugin.configSchema).length > 0 ? { configSchema: plugin.configSchema } : {}),
  ...(icons?.iconSvg ? { iconSvg: icons.iconSvg } : {}),
  ...(icons?.iconInactiveSvg ? { iconInactiveSvg: icons.iconInactiveSvg } : {}),
  ...(icons?.iconDarkSvg ? { iconDarkSvg: icons.iconDarkSvg } : {}),
  ...(icons?.iconDarkInactiveSvg ? { iconDarkInactiveSvg: icons.iconDarkInactiveSvg } : {}),
  tools: generateToolsManifest(plugin),
});
```
Replace with (added preScript parameter and conditional spread):
```ts
const generateManifest = (
  plugin: OpenTabsPlugin,
  sdkVersion: string,
  icons?: IconResult,
  preScript?: { file: string; hash: string },
): PluginManifestOutput => ({
  sdkVersion,
  ...(plugin.configSchema && Object.keys(plugin.configSchema).length > 0 ? { configSchema: plugin.configSchema } : {}),
  ...(icons?.iconSvg ? { iconSvg: icons.iconSvg } : {}),
  ...(icons?.iconInactiveSvg ? { iconInactiveSvg: icons.iconInactiveSvg } : {}),
  ...(icons?.iconDarkSvg ? { iconDarkSvg: icons.iconDarkSvg } : {}),
  ...(icons?.iconDarkInactiveSvg ? { iconDarkInactiveSvg: icons.iconDarkInactiveSvg } : {}),
  ...(preScript ? { preScriptFile: preScript.file, preScriptHash: preScript.hash } : {}),
  tools: generateToolsManifest(plugin),
});
```

Changes — OpenTabsRuntime (around line 740):
Existing:
```ts
interface OpenTabsRuntime {
  _readinessNonce?: string;
  _notifyReadinessChanged?: () => void;
  _navigationInterceptor?: NavigationInterceptor;
}
```
Add two optional fields at the end of the interface:
```ts
  /** The currently-executing plugin adapter's name.
   *  Set by each tool-handler wrap before calling user code so
   *  getPreScriptValue() reads the correct per-plugin namespace. */
  _pluginName?: string;
  preScript?: Record<string, Record<string, unknown>>;
```

Changes — tool.handle wrap (around line 855-875):
Inside the generated adapter IIFE string, the handler wrap currently begins with:
```ts
  const origHandle = tool.handle;
  tool.handle = async function(...handleArgs: [unknown, ...unknown[]]) {
    const startTime = performance.now();
    if (hasLifecycleHooks && typeof plugin.onToolInvocationStart === 'function') {
```
Insert two lines between startTime and the lifecycle-hook check:
```ts
    // Pin the current plugin name so getPreScriptValue() in the SDK reads
    // this plugin's bucket, not another adapter's.
    const runtime = globalThis.__openTabs!;
    runtime._pluginName = ${name};
```
Where `${name}` is the existing template variable bound earlier in the wrapper as `const name = JSON.stringify(plugin.name);`. If that binding isn't already present in this scope, add it at the top of the wrapper generation block alongside existing JSON.stringify literals.

Changes — bundlePreScript helper (new function, add above runBuild):
Copy the entire function verbatim from the POC (platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts lines 1004-1084 in the POC diff). The key behaviors:
- Creates a UUID-randomized wrapper .ts file in outDir
- Wrapper declares global __openTabs with _preScriptRunner + preScript
- Wrapper resets the per-plugin bucket on every load (pre-scripts are observational; each navigation gets a clean slate)
- Wrapper builds log.{debug,info,warn,error} with the `[opentabs:${PLUGIN_NAME}:pre-script]` prefix
- Wrapper installs _preScriptRunner, then imports the user module (which triggers definePreScript → _preScriptRunner), then deletes _preScriptRunner
- Uses esbuild with format:'iife', platform:'browser', bundle:true, minify:false, sourcemap:'linked', and the existing stripNodeBuiltins plugin
- finally block unlinks the wrapper file, swallowing any unlink error

Changes — runBuild call site (around line 1296 in the POC):
Locate the existing block that calls `const sdkVersion = await resolveSdkVersion(projectDir);`. Immediately after it and before the Step 6 comment / generateManifest call, insert the preScript bundling step. Copy verbatim from the POC — sets preScriptInfo = { file: PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME, hash } when bundlePreScript succeeded. The source-existence check throws 'Declared preScript source not found: ${path}. Check the "opentabs.preScript" field in package.json.' when the file is missing. The hash is computed by reading the built IIFE, stripping the trailing `\n//# sourceMappingURL=...\n?$` line, and taking sha256 hex. Then pass preScriptInfo as the fourth arg to generateManifest.

Constraints:
- Do NOT inline the pre-script inside the adapter IIFE — it MUST be a separate file at dist/pre-script.iife.js because chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts requires a file path, not a string body.
- Do NOT add stripNodeBuiltins conditionally — always include it, same as adapter bundling. Pre-scripts that accidentally try to import node: built-ins should fail loudly at build time.
- The _pluginName pin must happen BEFORE onToolInvocationStart, not inside it — onToolInvocationStart is user code and must not be able to read the wrong bucket if it calls getPreScriptValue.
- Keep `minify: false` in bundlePreScript. Minification makes pre-script behavior harder to review during plugin_inspect (US-006).
- Do NOT introduce a second template-string generator for the pre-script wrapper — the current runBuild wrapper string pattern (triple backticks with escaped ${...}) is deliberately low-ceremony because each adapter has its own wrapper; bundlePreScript is the only new code that writes wrapper JS.

userStories.title: MCP server: loader reads preScript IIFE content, state/registry carry it, adapter-files writes content-hashed pre-script files, extension-protocol threads preScriptFile/preScriptHash into sync.full and plugin.update
userStories.description: Port the server-side wiring from the POC: RegisteredPlugin gains preScript and preScriptHash fields, loadPlugin reads dist/pre-script.iife.js into memory when tools.json declares preScriptFile, writePreScriptFile writes <plugin>-prescript-<hash8>.js into ~/.opentabs/extension/adapters/ (distinct filename pattern from adapter files), cleanupStaleAdapterFiles understands the -prescript- suffix, and both sendSyncFull and sendPluginUpdate include preScriptFile/preScriptHash in the extension payload.
acceptanceCriteria: LoadedPlugin and RegisteredPlugin have readonly preScript: string | undefined and readonly preScriptHash: string | undefined

loadPlugin reads dist/pre-script.iife.js when tools.json has preScriptFile, populates preScript + preScriptHash on the returned LoadedPlugin, and logs a warning 'declares preScriptFile in manifest but pre-script.iife.js is missing at <path> — pre-script disabled' when the file is absent (does NOT fail the load)

writePreScriptFile in adapter-files.ts writes a file named `<pluginName>-prescript-<hash8>.js` via atomicWrite and cleans up any earlier `<pluginName>-prescript-<other-hash8>.js` files for the same plugin

cleanupStaleAdapterFiles's stripHash helper strips both the `-[0-9a-f]{8}` suffix AND the `-prescript` suffix before checking against currentPluginNames, so pre-script files for known plugins are not deleted
writeAllAdapterFiles returns Map<string, { adapterFile: string; preScriptFile?: string }> and writes the pre-script alongside the adapter in parallel per plugin
sendSyncFull includes preScriptFile (from the map) and preScriptHash (from the plugin) in each plugin payload via conditional spreads
sendPluginUpdate calls writePreScriptFile when the updated plugin has preScript, includes preScriptFile and preScriptHash in the plugin.update JSON-RPC payload

userStories.title: Browser extension: pre-script-registration module with syncPreScripts/upsertPreScript/removePreScript, wired into message-router and PluginMeta, validated against safe filename pattern
userStories.description: Port platform/browser-extension/src/pre-script-registration.ts from the POC but with an added filename validation guard (the one production gap the POC acknowledged). Wire preScriptFile/preScriptHash into PluginMeta and ValidatedPluginPayload in message-router.ts, and call syncPreScripts in handleSyncFull, upsertPreScript in handlePluginUpdate, removePreScript in handlePluginUninstall.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/browser-extension/src/pre-script-registration.ts exports syncPreScripts, upsertPreScript, removePreScript
registrationId(pluginName) returns `opentabs-pre-${pluginName}` and this prefix is the only scanner key used in getRegisteredPreScriptIds

upsertPreScript rejects (returns early with console.warn) when preScriptFile does NOT match /^adapters\/[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*-prescript-[0-9a-f]{8}\.js$/ — prevents path traversal and prevents registering an arbitrary JS path
upsertPreScript calls chrome.scripting.unregisterContentScripts before registerContentScripts so re-registration always succeeds

registerContentScripts params: matches=meta.urlPatterns, excludeMatches=meta.excludePatterns, js=[meta.preScriptFile], runAt='document_start', world='MAIN', persistAcrossSessions=true, allFrames=false
removePreScript(pluginName) calls unregisterContentScripts({ ids: [registrationId(pluginName)] }) and swallows errors
syncPreScripts computes expected + current IDs, unregisters stale opentabs-pre-* IDs not in the expected set, then upserts each plugin in parallel
ValidatedPluginPayload in message-router.ts has preScriptFile and preScriptHash, and validatePluginPayload carries them through
toPluginMeta copies preScriptFile and preScriptHash from ValidatedPluginPayload to PluginMeta
PluginMeta in extension-messages.ts has preScriptFile and preScriptHash optional string fields
handleSyncFull calls `await syncPreScripts(metas)` after storePluginsBatch
handlePluginUpdate calls `await upsertPreScript(meta)` after storePluginsBatch and before injectPluginIntoMatchingTabs
handlePluginUninstall calls `await removePreScript(pluginName)` unconditionally (safe even if no registration exists)

userStories.title: Include pre-script source in plugin_inspect output so the plugin review system covers pre-scripts
userStories.description: Extend handlePluginInspect in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts so the tool result includes the pre-script IIFE alongside the adapter IIFE. Without this, a plugin could ship malicious code in pre-script.iife.js that runs at document_start in MAIN world and never gets reviewed — pre-scripts have strictly higher privilege than adapters (earlier execution, bypasses page CSP) so they MUST be part of the review surface.
acceptanceCriteria: handlePluginInspect response object includes preScriptSource (string or omitted), preScriptLineCount (number or omitted), and preScriptByteSize (number or omitted) fields next to adapterSource/lineCount/byteSize
When plugin.preScript is undefined, none of the preScript* fields appear in the response
When plugin.preScript is a non-empty string, preScriptSource equals plugin.preScript and the line/byte counts are computed the same way as the adapter

REVIEW_GUIDANCE text in mcp-tool-dispatch.ts explicitly instructs reviewers to audit preScriptSource with special scrutiny (runs at document_start in MAIN world, can intercept fetch/XHR, can read auth state before the page hides it)
No changes to handlePluginMarkReviewed — the review token already covers the plugin as a whole
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts (extend handlePluginInspect response and REVIEW_GUIDANCE)

Context: The plugin review system already blocks plugins from running until an agent + user have reviewed the adapter IIFE source. Pre-scripts run EARLIER and with a strictly broader capability surface (document_start MAIN world, before page CSP applies) — so they MUST be part of the review payload. Without this story a reviewed plugin can still ship un-reviewed pre-script code, which is exactly the attack the review system is supposed to prevent.

Read first:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts lines 640-722 (REVIEW_GUIDANCE + handlePluginInspect)
- platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts RegisteredPlugin (plugin.preScript + plugin.preScriptHash already plumbed in US-004)

Changes — handlePluginInspect (around line 646-721):
The current function computes `const adapterSource = plugin.iife;`, validates it, then computes `const lineCount = adapterSource.split('\n').length;` and `const byteSize = Buffer.byteLength(adapterSource, 'utf-8');` and builds a response object. Extend the response:

After the `const byteSize = Buffer.byteLength(adapterSource, 'utf-8');` line, add:
```ts
  const preScriptSource = plugin.preScript;
  const preScriptLineCount = preScriptSource ? preScriptSource.split('\n').length : undefined;
  const preScriptByteSize = preScriptSource ? Buffer.byteLength(preScriptSource, 'utf-8') : undefined;
```

Extend the response object literal. The existing response builder spreads conditional fields already (author, npmPackage). Add three more conditional spreads BEFORE `reviewGuidance: REVIEW_GUIDANCE,`:
```ts
    ...(preScriptSource ? { preScriptSource } : {}),
    ...(preScriptLineCount !== undefined ? { preScriptLineCount } : {}),
    ...(preScriptByteSize !== undefined ? { preScriptByteSize } : {}),
```

Changes — REVIEW_GUIDANCE constant (around line 600):
The existing guidance text lives as a multi-line template string. Append a new section immediately before the closing backtick (keep the existing top-level 'Do not downplay concerns...' line last):
```

If preScriptSource is present, REVIEW IT WITH SPECIAL SCRUTINY:
- Pre-scripts run at document_start in MAIN world, strictly before any page
  JavaScript — they have earlier execution than the adapter and can observe
  state the adapter cannot (e.g., auth tokens in the first outbound fetch,
  early globals the page may later hide).
- Pre-scripts cannot call chrome.* APIs and have no tool runtime, but they
  CAN monkey-patch window.fetch, window.XMLHttpRequest, window.WebSocket,
  document.cookie, Request/Response headers, and any global the page exposes.
- A malicious pre-script can silently exfiltrate bearer tokens, CSRF
  nonces, session cookies, or request bodies by installing its own fetch
  wrapper that forwards to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
- Verify: (1) every set(key, value) call stashes only data the plugin
  legitimately needs, (2) no network calls escape the page's own origin
  other than those originally made by the page, (3) no data is written
  to document.cookie, localStorage, or any global the page didn't already
  expose.
```
Do NOT move the existing `Do not downplay concerns...` line — it stays last.

Constraints:
- Do NOT add a separate review token for pre-scripts. One token per plugin covers both surfaces; the reviewer is expected to audit everything returned by a single plugin_inspect call.
- Do NOT include preScriptHash in the response — the hash is for runtime change detection, not for human review.
- Do NOT treat a plugin with preScript but no adapter iife as valid — the existing `if (!adapterSource || adapterSource.length === 0)` early-return still fires. Every plugin must ship an adapter; pre-scripts are additive.
- The response JSON serialization must keep preScript* fields near adapterSource — both are stringified by `JSON.stringify(response, null, 2)` so field order matches insertion order. Keep them contiguous for readability in the reviewer's terminal.
- Do NOT write a preScript-specific REVIEW_GUIDANCE constant. The one constant serves both the adapter and the pre-script so AI reviewers get unified instructions in a single prompt.

userStories.title: Re-register pre-scripts on chrome.runtime.onInstalled and onStartup, and auto-reload matching tabs on preScriptHash change
userStories.description: Close the biggest POC gap: `chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts` with persistAcrossSessions is NOT guaranteed to survive extension updates or fresh browser starts, so on both lifecycle events the extension must re-sync pre-script registrations from the stored PluginMeta. Also: when handlePluginUpdate sees a preScriptHash change compared to the previous stored meta, auto-reload all matching already-open tabs so the new pre-script takes effect (otherwise stale tabs run with the old pre-script until the user navigates away).
acceptanceCriteria: platform/browser-extension/src/background.ts imports syncPreScripts from ./pre-script-registration.js
The onInstalled handler's async IIFE awaits syncPreScripts(Object.values(await getAllPluginMeta())) after reinjectStoredPlugins
The onStartup handler's async IIFE awaits syncPreScripts(Object.values(await getAllPluginMeta())) after reinjectStoredPlugins

platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts handlePluginUpdate compares newMeta.preScriptHash against the previous meta (via getPluginMeta) BEFORE storePluginsBatch; when the hash differs AND previous.preScriptFile was set, it reloads all matching tabs via chrome.tabs.reload after upsertPreScript
The reload path uses queryMatchingTabIds (already exported from iife-injection.ts) to find matching tabs and calls chrome.tabs.reload on each, swallowing per-tab errors with console.warn
When preScriptHash is unchanged or preScriptFile was unset before, no tab reload occurs

New registration (previous meta had no preScriptFile, new meta has one) does NOT auto-reload — document the reasoning in a comment: forcing a reload on first-install would surprise the user; the registration will take effect on the user's next navigation

userStories.title: Side panel: hasPreScript badge on PluginCard when the plugin ships a document_start pre-script
userStories.description: Plumb a hasPreScript boolean from ConfigStatePlugin (server-side) through the sync payload to the side-panel PluginCard, and render a small tooltip-backed icon next to the plugin name so users can see which plugins run code at document_start in MAIN world. Reviewable code at document_start is a privileged capability and users should have visible awareness.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/shared/src/index.ts ConfigStatePlugin has hasPreScript: boolean
platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts serializePluginForExtension returns hasPreScript: plugin.preScript !== undefined
platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts buildConfigStatePayload passes hasPreScript through to ConfigStatePlugin entries

platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx renders a FileCode icon (lucide-react) with a Tooltip.Content text 'This plugin runs a pre-script at document_start in MAIN world' next to the ShieldQuestionMark, visible only when plugin.hasPreScript is true
The icon uses `h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground align-middle inline-block` matching ShieldQuestionMark's sizing
Browser tools and plugins without pre-scripts never render the icon
userStories.notes: Files: - platform/shared/src/index.ts (extend ConfigStatePlugin) - platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (populate hasPreScript) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx (render icon) Context: A pre-script is a privileged capability — it runs BEFORE any page JavaScript in MAIN world, bypassing page CSP. Users deserve a passive visual indicator (no modal, no dialog) so they can distinguish plugins that merely automate page interactions from plugins that install interceptors at document_start. Read first: - platform/shared/src/index.ts ConfigStatePlugin interface (around line 324-361) - platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts serializePluginForExtension (line 152-212) and buildConfigStatePayload (line 352+) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx lines 240-267 (the existing ShieldQuestionMark render) Changes — shared/src/index.ts: ConfigStatePlugin already has a `reviewed: boolean` field and various optional SVG icon fields. Add `hasPreScript` alongside `reviewed`: ```ts /** Whether this plugin's current version has been reviewed (reviewedVersion matches installed version) */ reviewed: boolean; /** Whether this plugin declares a pre-script that runs at document_start in MAIN world. */ hasPreScript: boolean; ``` Changes — extension-handlers.ts serializePluginForExtension: Current return shape includes `reviewed: pluginConfig?.reviewedVersion === plugin.version,`. Add right after it: ```ts hasPreScript: plugin.preScript !== undefined, ``` Also update the function's return type signature to include `hasPreScript: boolean;`. Changes — extension-handlers.ts buildConfigStatePayload: This function already maps RegisteredPlugin → ConfigStatePlugin via serializePluginForExtension spread. Because serialize already returns hasPreScript, the spread carries it. Verify by reading the function body — if it builds an object literal that EXPLICITLY enumerates the fields from serializePluginForExtension, add hasPreScript to that enumeration. If it spreads, the spread already covers it. Changes — PluginCard.tsx: Current import line (~line 5): ```ts import { ChevronDown, Settings, ShieldQuestionMark } from 'lucide-react'; ``` Add FileCode alphabetically: ```ts import { ChevronDown, FileCode, Settings, ShieldQuestionMark } from 'lucide-react'; ``` Inside the plugin-name row (around line 253-260), immediately AFTER the `{!plugin.reviewed && ( ... )}` block and BEFORE the closing `` of the name row, add: ```tsx {plugin.hasPreScript && ( This plugin runs a pre-script at document_start in MAIN world )} ``` Constraints: - Do NOT make the icon clickable or actionable — this is a passive awareness affordance. No modal, no expanded details. If users want more info, they use plugin_inspect. - Do NOT hide the icon when the plugin is disabled or unreviewed. Visibility of the pre-script capability is independent of the permission/review state — the icon shows a structural fact about the plugin's manifest. - Do NOT change browser-tool rendering. Browser tools are not plugins and have no preScript field; the `plugin.hasPreScript` expression is only evaluated on real plugin entries. - Do NOT alter the Tooltip primitive or its theme — the existing ShieldQuestionMark tooltip is the reference for sizing and color. FileCode uses identical Tailwind classes. - Do NOT add a test to PluginCard.stories.tsx in this story — Storybook stories are covered by the existing retro/plugin-card pattern and adding one here is out of scope.

userStories.title: Port the pre-script test plugin (plugins/prescript-test) and its mock server (e2e/prescript-mock-server.ts) to main
userStories.description: Copy plugins/prescript-test/* and e2e/prescript-mock-server.ts from the POC branch verbatim. The test plugin is the minimum plugin needed to exercise the pre-script pipeline end-to-end: it declares opentabs.preScript, its pre-script.ts patches window.fetch to capture bearer tokens, and its echo_auth tool reads the captured value via getPreScriptValue. The mock server simulates PR #69's failure mode — an inline <script> in <head> that fires an authenticated fetch and immediately overwrites window.fetch.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/prescript-test/package.json declares opentabs.preScript='src/pre-script.ts', urlPatterns=['http://localhost/*','http://127.0.0.1/*'], and opentabs-plugin build as its build script
plugins/prescript-test/src/pre-script.ts calls definePreScript(({ set, log }) => { ... }) and imports from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script' (NOT from the main SDK)

plugins/prescript-test/src/tools/echo-auth.ts exports an echoAuth tool defined via defineTool with input z.object({}) and output z.object({ token, authUrl, capturedAt, source }), and reads values via getPreScriptValue from the main SDK
plugins/prescript-test/src/index.ts default-exports a new PreScriptTestPlugin() whose isReady() returns true
plugins/prescript-test/tsconfig.json extends the plugin-sdk base config (or equivalent) and compiles src/
plugins/prescript-test/package.json has npm run build that runs tsc && opentabs-plugin build
plugins/prescript-test/package-lock.json is committed with resolved workspace dependencies (file:../../platform/plugin-sdk etc.)

e2e/prescript-mock-server.ts listens on 127.0.0.1, accepts PORT=0, serves HTML with an inline <script> that fires fetch('/api/v2.0/me') with a random bearer token and then sets window.fetch = stub, exposes GET /control/server-info returning { token: <expected> }, and prints 'Listening on http://127.0.0.1:<port>' on startup
The mock server imports ./orphan-guard.js at the top (same guard every test server in e2e/ uses)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/prescript-test/package.json
- plugins/prescript-test/package-lock.json
- plugins/prescript-test/tsconfig.json
- plugins/prescript-test/src/index.ts
- plugins/prescript-test/src/pre-script.ts
- plugins/prescript-test/src/tools/echo-auth.ts
- e2e/prescript-mock-server.ts

Context: These files exist in the POC branch already and port verbatim — no design changes required. The test plugin is a sibling to plugins/e2e-test (not in npm workspaces — each plugin has its own isolated dependency tree). The mock server sits in e2e/ next to test-server.ts and follows the same subprocess-over-stdout port-announcement convention.

Port the files by checking out the content from poc/pre-script-feature@990d64cb — the diff is clean:
```bash
git show poc/pre-script-feature:plugins/prescript-test/package.json
git show poc/pre-script-feature:plugins/prescript-test/tsconfig.json
git show poc/pre-script-feature:plugins/prescript-test/src/index.ts
git show poc/pre-script-feature:plugins/prescript-test/src/pre-script.ts
git show poc/pre-script-feature:plugins/prescript-test/src/tools/echo-auth.ts
git show poc/pre-script-feature:plugins/prescript-test/package-lock.json
git show poc/pre-script-feature:e2e/prescript-mock-server.ts
```
The worker should run each of these and write the output to the corresponding new file. No content changes.

After writing the files, install plugin dependencies so the lockfile has valid content:
```bash
cd plugins/prescript-test && npm install --no-audit --no-fund --ignore-scripts
```
Then build the plugin so dist/adapter.iife.js and dist/pre-script.iife.js exist (E2E tests in US-010 expect them):
```bash
cd plugins/prescript-test && npm run build
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT add prescript-test to the root package.json workspaces field. All plugins/ entries are standalone, by project convention.
- Do NOT rename the plugin or the npm package name. The plugin name must be `prescript-test` (derived by plugin-tools from `@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-prescript-test`) so E2E tests can reference it with stable IDs like `prescript-test_echo_auth` and `opentabs-pre-prescript-test`.
- Do NOT import anything from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk inside src/pre-script.ts except via the subpath `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script`. The narrow subpath is deliberate — importing from the main entry would pull the tool runtime into a MAIN-world script that cannot use it.
- Do NOT register the plugin in ~/.opentabs/config.json during the build. Tests register it per-test via writeTestConfig.
- The mock server's PAGE_HTML must set `window.__pageBootstrapResult` in the .then() callback of the initial fetch — the E2E test (US-010) waits for that global to confirm the page has run its bootstrap before asserting.
- Do NOT change the `const EXPECTED_TOKEN = `outlook-mock-${Math.random()...};` generation — each process instance gets a unique token, which is what the mock's /control/server-info reports. Tests read the token from that endpoint; if you hardcode a token, tests can't verify end-to-end identity.
- Do NOT add biome config to plugins/prescript-test. The root biome config already covers plugins/* via the check:plugins script — look at plugins/e2e-test for reference (it has its own biome.json but this POC plugin can reuse the sibling pattern if needed; start by copying the POC structure verbatim and only add a biome.json if check:plugins fails).

userStories.title: Production E2E: pre-script captures bearer token before page overwrites window.fetch, using main-branch fixtures
userStories.description: Replace the POC e2e/prescript-poc.e2e.ts (which inlined its own fixture setup) with a production-grade Playwright test that uses the main-branch fixtures in the same shape as setupIsolatedIifeTest. The test installs plugins/prescript-test, opens the mock-server URL as a new tab, and asserts: (1) chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts reports opentabs-pre-prescript-test with runAt=document_start and world=MAIN, (2) globalThis.__openTabs.preScript['prescript-test'].authToken equals the mock server's expected token, (3) the echo_auth MCP tool dispatch returns { token: <expected>, source: 'pre-script' }, (4) the mock page DID execute its fetch stub overwrite (window.__fetchOverrideInstalled is true).
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/pre-script.e2e.ts is a new file that uses the shared `test` and helpers (not a bespoke fixture)

The test starts the mock server via a helper that spawns node --import tsx/esm e2e/prescript-mock-server.ts with PORT=0 and parses 'Listening on http://127.0.0.1:<port>' from stdout, then fetches /control/server-info for the expected token
The test uses writeTestConfig, startMcpServer, launchExtensionContext, and setupAdapterSymlink (all exported from e2e/fixtures.ts or e2e/helpers.ts) rather than reimplementing them
After plugin sync, the test waits 500ms OR polls until chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts includes opentabs-pre-prescript-test (with 5s timeout) before navigating
The test asserts chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts returns an entry for opentabs-pre-prescript-test with runAt='document_start', world='MAIN'
After navigating to the mock URL, page.evaluate returns __pageBootstrapResult !== undefined and __fetchOverrideInstalled === true (proving the page ran its hostile bootstrap)
page.evaluate reads globalThis.__openTabs.preScript['prescript-test'] and asserts authToken equals the expected token from /control/server-info
callToolExpectSuccess for prescript-test_echo_auth returns { token: <expected>, source: 'pre-script' }
The test file mirrors the cleanup pattern of setupIsolatedIifeTest — all spawned subprocesses and browser contexts are torn down in a finally block
The test runs in under 15 seconds on a warm machine (POC ran in 3.3s; budget for CI variance)

userStories.title: E2E: pre-script registration survives extension restart (validates onStartup re-sync)
userStories.description: Regression test for US-007. Install the pre-script-test plugin, confirm opentabs-pre-prescript-test is registered, then force the extension to restart via chrome.runtime.reload() from the background service worker and re-assert the registration is back. This proves syncPreScripts actually fires on the onStartup lifecycle event (Chrome's persistAcrossSessions is unreliable).
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/pre-script-restart.e2e.ts (new) installs the prescript-test plugin exactly like US-010, confirms opentabs-pre-prescript-test is registered

The test then triggers chrome.runtime.reload() via the background service worker's evaluate, waits for the service worker to become available again (with a generous 30s timeout), and re-fetches chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts
After reload, the test asserts opentabs-pre-prescript-test is registered AGAIN with runAt='document_start' and world='MAIN'
A fresh navigation to the mock URL after reload still captures the bearer token (end-to-end validation of the re-registration path)
All cleanup happens in a finally block consistent with US-010

userStories.title: E2E: plugin.uninstall cleans up pre-script registration and plugin.update auto-reloads matching tabs on hash change
userStories.description: Two regression tests covering the two lifecycle tails: (a) after plugin.uninstall, opentabs-pre-prescript-test is no longer in chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts; (b) when the plugin's pre-script source changes and a plugin.update fires, already-open matching tabs get reloaded automatically so the new pre-script takes effect.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/pre-script-lifecycle.e2e.ts (new) has two test cases: 'uninstall removes the registered pre-script' and 'plugin.update with changed preScriptHash reloads matching tabs'

The uninstall test installs prescript-test, confirms opentabs-pre-prescript-test is registered, calls the MCP plugin.uninstall JSON-RPC via the server's control API (or the equivalent helper in helpers.ts), and polls chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts until opentabs-pre-prescript-test is absent (5s timeout)

The hash-change test installs prescript-test, opens the mock page, records the tab id, modifies plugins/prescript-test/src/pre-script.ts (change a log message or a captured field name) to force a content change, runs `opentabs-plugin build` (or its in-process equivalent) so the file watcher fires plugin.update, and asserts the tab got reloaded by either (a) listening for page.waitForEvent('load') with a reasonable timeout, or (b) polling a sentinel like the tab's performance.timing.navigationStart changing
Both tests clean up spawned subprocesses, browser contexts, and modified plugin sources in finally blocks (restore the original pre-script.ts content)
Both tests budget under 30s

userStories.title: E2E: extension rejects plugin.update payload with malformed preScriptFile path (validates US-005 filename regex)
userStories.description: Regression test for the PRE_SCRIPT_FILE_PATTERN guard in upsertPreScript. Inject a forged plugin.update into the background via chrome.runtime.sendMessage (or by directly calling message-router's handler from the service worker) with preScriptFile='../../../etc/passwd', preScriptFile='some-other-adapter.js', or preScriptFile='adapters/..%2Fevil.js', and assert that the malformed payload does NOT result in a new chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts entry.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/pre-script-payload-validation.e2e.ts (new) installs prescript-test normally, records the current registered-script ids

The test calls sw.evaluate(() => { /* send a plugin.update message with malformed preScriptFile */ }) — the simplest route is to dispatch from within the service worker directly so it bypasses the WebSocket validation layer and exercises only the extension-side filename guard
After the malformed message is processed, chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts is identical to the pre-malformed snapshot (no new registrations, no changes)
The test verifies console.warn contains the string 'refusing to register pre-script with unexpected filename' — use sw.evaluate to attach a listener before the forged message
Three malformed cases covered: (a) path traversal '../../../etc/passwd', (b) no -prescript- segment 'adapters/plugin-abcdef12.js', (c) wrong subdirectory 'evil/prescript-test-prescript-a1b2c3d4.js'

userStories.title: Document the pre-script feature in plugin-sdk, browser-extension, mcp-server, and shared CLAUDE.md files
userStories.description: Add documentation for the pre-script feature to the four package-level CLAUDE.md files so future contributors and AI agents understand the capability without re-reading the poc branch.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md has a ## Pre-Script section that documents the `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script` subpath, definePreScript signature, and getPreScriptValue reader — including the 'undefined is a normal branch' contract

platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md has a ## Pre-Script Registration section documenting the syncPreScripts / upsertPreScript / removePreScript module, the filename validation regex, the onInstalled/onStartup re-sync, and the auto-reload-on-hash-change behavior

platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md has a ## Pre-Scripts section documenting how loader reads pre-script content, how writePreScriptFile emits content-hashed files, how sync.full/plugin.update thread preScriptFile/preScriptHash, and how plugin_inspect includes preScriptSource
platform/shared/CLAUDE.md mentions PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME alongside ADAPTER_FILENAME in its constants list
No marketing prose — documentation follows the existing CLAUDE.md tone (terse, technical, facts-over-explanation)
No net-new architecture docs at the root — the existing root CLAUDE.md 'Key Concepts' section may gain one line referencing pre-scripts, but it must stay plugin-agnostic and high-level
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md
- platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md
- platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md
- platform/shared/CLAUDE.md
- CLAUDE.md (root — one-line mention in Key Concepts)

Context: Documentation is the final story in the PRD. All behavior is already implemented and tested by US-001 through US-013. This story just writes the docs that describe what already exists.

Read first the existing CLAUDE.md files to match the tone:
- platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md — has sections like '## Tool Definitions', '## Lifecycle Hooks'. Add '## Pre-Script' at an appropriate place (alongside 'Progress Reporting' or similar).
- platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md — has sections like '## Tab State Machine', '## Tool Dispatch'. Add '## Pre-Script Registration' next to Tool Dispatch.
- platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md — has sections like '## Plugin Discovery', '## Plugin Review System'. Add '## Pre-Scripts' next to Plugin Review System (they are related security concerns).
- platform/shared/CLAUDE.md — short file, add PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME to the existing constants mention.

Draft content (planner's draft — worker should refine to match each file's voice):

platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md — new section:
```md
## Pre-Script

Plugins may declare an optional pre-script that runs at `document_start` in MAIN world, strictly before any page JavaScript. Pre-scripts are for observing or patching page runtime state that the page would otherwise hide — auth tokens in the first outbound fetch, CSRF nonces, early globals.

Declare the pre-script source path in `package.json`:
```
"opentabs": { "preScript": "src/pre-script.ts", ... }
```

Import `definePreScript` from the narrow subpath `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script` (the main SDK entry is not available at document_start — no DOM helpers, no tool runtime, no Zod):
```ts
import { definePreScript } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script';

definePreScript(({ set, log }) => {
  const originalFetch = window.fetch.bind(window);
  window.fetch = async (input, init) => {
    // intercept and stash auth, then call originalFetch
    const headers = new Headers(init?.headers);
    const auth = headers.get('Authorization');
    if (auth?.startsWith('Bearer ')) set('authToken', auth.slice(7));
    return originalFetch(input, init);
  };
});
```

Read stashed values from the adapter via `getPreScriptValue` (from the main SDK):
```ts
import { getPreScriptValue } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';
const token = getPreScriptValue<string>('authToken'); // undefined is a normal branch
```

`undefined` is a normal return — it indicates the adapter was injected into a tab that was already open before the plugin registered (pre-scripts only fire on future navigations), or the pre-script ran but didn't call `set` for that key. Always handle it.

Pre-scripts execute once per navigation and start each page load with a fresh bucket.
```

platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md — new section:
```md
## Pre-Script Registration

Pre-scripts are registered via `chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts` with `runAt: 'document_start'`, `world: 'MAIN'`, `persistAcrossSessions: true`. The registration id is `opentabs-pre-<pluginName>`. Source lives in `src/pre-script-registration.ts`:

- `syncPreScripts(metas)` — called on sync.full and on extension lifecycle events; reconciles the registered set against the plugin metadata index, unregistering any orphaned `opentabs-pre-*` entries and upserting each plugin's current pre-script.
- `upsertPreScript(meta)` — unregisters then registers a single plugin's pre-script. Validates meta.preScriptFile against `/^adapters\/[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*-prescript-[0-9a-f]{8}\.js$/` and refuses unexpected filenames.
- `removePreScript(pluginName)` — unregisters the plugin's pre-script; safe if never registered.

`chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts` with `persistAcrossSessions` does NOT reliably survive extension updates or cold starts. `background.ts` calls `syncPreScripts` in both `onInstalled` and `onStartup` handlers, and on the top-level service-worker wake-up path, reading from the durable `chrome.storage.local` plugin metadata index.

When `plugin.update` arrives with a changed `preScriptHash`, `message-router.ts` also reloads all matching already-open tabs via `chrome.tabs.reload` — Chrome's registered content scripts only fire on future navigations, so existing tabs would otherwise run stale pre-script code until the next user navigation. First-time registrations do NOT auto-reload (avoids surprising the user mid-task).
```

platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md — new section:
```md
## Pre-Scripts

Plugins may declare a pre-script that the extension registers at `document_start` in MAIN world. The MCP server's role in the pipeline:

- **`loader.ts`**: reads `dist/pre-script.iife.js` into `LoadedPlugin.preScript` when `dist/tools.json` declares `preScriptFile`. Missing file is a warn + degrade (pre-script disabled, plugin still loads).
- **`adapter-files.ts`**: `writePreScriptFile` emits `<plugin>-prescript-<hash8>.js` into `~/.opentabs/extension/adapters/` (distinct filename from the `<plugin>-<hash8>.js` adapter files; content-hashed so `chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts` always sees a fresh path on content change). `cleanupStaleAdapterFiles` strips both the hash and the `-prescript` suffix when checking staleness.
- **`extension-protocol.ts`**: `sync.full` and `plugin.update` JSON-RPC payloads include `preScriptFile` (relative path under the extension root) and `preScriptHash` (for change detection on the extension side).
- **`plugin_inspect`** (`mcp-tool-dispatch.ts`): the response includes `preScriptSource`, `preScriptLineCount`, and `preScriptByteSize` alongside `adapterSource` when the plugin declares a pre-script. The `REVIEW_GUIDANCE` constant instructs reviewers to audit pre-scripts with special scrutiny — they run earlier and with broader capability than adapters.
```

platform/shared/CLAUDE.md — add one line to the constants list (look at the existing list and add `PRE_SCRIPT_FILENAME` next to `ADAPTER_FILENAME` alphabetically).

Root CLAUDE.md — in the 'Key Concepts' section, add ONE line (keep it plugin-agnostic):
```md
**Pre-scripts**: plugins may declare an optional pre-script that runs at `document_start` in MAIN world via `chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts`, strictly before any page JavaScript. See `platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md` for the plugin-author API.
```

Constraints:
- Keep the CLAUDE.md tone: direct, technical, no marketing. Reuse the existing file's voice.
- DO NOT duplicate content across the four package docs — each file covers its own slice (SDK = plugin-author API, extension = registration module, server = pipeline, shared = constants).
- DO NOT add documentation to any file other than these five. No README updates, no docs/ site updates.
- DO NOT mention the POC branch, PR #69, or MSAL specifics in the CLAUDE.md files — those are motivating-example details that belong in the PRD, not in architectural docs.
- The root CLAUDE.md addition is ONE LINE. Do not expand.
